hyperedges.label: Local AI Hub and Model Orchestration Subsystem
Licensing and Clock Rollback Entitlement Flow

nodes.label: ADR-001: Frontend Framework & State Management Choice
nodes.rationale: Removed Zustand and React dependencies. All state management handled via Svelte 5 Runes ($state, $derived) and state modules to minimize memory footprint and bundle size in WKWebView.
nodes.norm_label: adr-001: frontend framework & state management choice

nodes.label: ADR-002: Local Vector Store Selection
nodes.rationale: Selected LanceDB via native Rust lancedb crate (with sqlite-vec fallback). Operates directly within Tauri binary with Metal SIMD hardware acceleration on Apple Silicon without requiring a separate server.

nodes.label: ADR-003: Knowledge Graph Technology
nodes.rationale: Embedded property graph stored inside SQLite using standard graph_node and graph_edge tables, traversed via recursive CTEs. Demoted LadybugDB to Phase 2 to avoid pre-1.0 C++ bindings.

nodes.label: ADR-004: Embedded Browser Strategy
nodes.rationale: Use Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) via chromiumoxide to connect to user's existing Chrome browser, with Obscura (Rust headless browser) as offline fallback. Avoids bundling heavy Chromium Embedded Framework.

nodes.label: ADR-005: Sync & Client-Side Conflict Resolution
nodes.rationale: Deferred Loro CRDT multi-device sync to Phase 3. Phase 1 provides simple manual backup export (SQLite dump + LanceDB vectors) and restore.

nodes.label: ADR-006: Offline-First Subscription & Entitlement Model
nodes.rationale: Uses Rust-based EntitlementService for offline JWT validation using a compile-time embedded RSA public key. Detects clock rollback using secure timestamps in Keychain.

nodes.label: ADR-007: Local Agent Runtime Integration Architecture
nodes.rationale: Spawns Node.js process sidecar running Pi SDK. Rust communicates with sidecar via JSON-RPC 2.0 over standard I/O (stdio). Avoids rewriting TypeScript agent SDK in Rust.
nodes.norm_label: adr-007: local agent runtime integration architecture

nodes.label: ADR-008: Database Architecture & Tauri SQL Plugin Exclusion
nodes.rationale: Excludes tauri-plugin-sql to prevent dual pool conflicts and WAL locking. Rust backend manages SQLite directly via rusqlite, exposing commands to Svelte frontend.
nodes.norm_label: adr-008: database architecture & tauri sql plugin exclusion

nodes.label: ssr
nodes.rationale: Tauri requires SPA mode (ssr = false) because it lacks a Node.js server for SSR.
